MetaEnum.description:
SyncFromSource - Synchonization happens from source Att0MeasurableAttribute objects to input Att0MeasurableAttribute objects.
PublishToSource - Synchonization happens from output Att0MeasurableAttribute objects to source Att0MeasurableAttribute objects.
MetaEnum.name: SyncDirection

ParameterInfo.description: The list of measurable attributes and their parent objects.

Operation.description:
This operation deletes measurable attribute (Att0MeasurableAttribute) objects from given parent object.
    • If the given measurable attribute(Att0MeasurableAttribute) is a source attribute, it is is deleted directly.
    • If the given measurable attribute(Att0MeasurableAttribute) is an overridden attribute, the overridden attribute itself is deleted as well as its related source attribute.
    • If the given measurable attribute(Att0MeasurableAttribute) is a source attribute and it is assigned as Input/Output attribute to an Analysis Request(Crt0VldnContractRevision), then the delete actions is discarded and a warning message is returned.
Operation.name: deleteMeasurableAttributes
Operation.returnDescription:
A map of client identifier to the Att0MeasurableAttribute objects that could not be deleted. The following partial errors may be returned:
    • 185204 - A valid parent object must be specified.
    • 185205 - A value for the measurable attribute parameter must be provided in order to complete the "Delete" operation.
    • 185206 - The measurable attribute could not be deleted, because it is referenced in the Analysis Request.
    • 185207 - The measurable attribute could not be deleted, because it is referenced in other context.
    • 185208 - The measurable attribute could not be deleted, because it is referenced.
Operation.useCase:
• A system architect creates a product structure of logical blocks (Fnd0LogicalBlock), and defines for each block its measurable attributes (Att0MeasurableAttribute). A system designer may override measurable attribute in context of product. As the design of the product architecture evolves or need to re-design, the system designer may need to delete the already created measurable attributes from product architecture and will re-create measurable attribute.
    • A system architect may revise the logical block (Fnd0LogicalBlock), it copies measurable attributes from previous logical block revision (Fnd0LogicalBlockRevision) to new revision block. A system designer may want to delete attributes from previous logical block revision, and re-create attributes (Att0MeasurableAttribute) in the new logical block revision.

ParameterInfo.description: Input information required for the getAttributeComplexData in the form of GetAttributeComplexDataIn.

Operation.description: This operation gets the complex data for the given Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute.
Operation.name: getAttributeComplexData
Operation.returnDescription:
The operation returns two maps attrPropNamesToAttrComplexDataMap and measurePropNamesToMeasurementComplexDataMap corresponding to each input property names vector of attrPropNames and measurePropNames respectively along with the ServiceData. The attrPropNamesToAttrComplexDataMap maps the attrPropNames properties to its list of complex data whereas measurePropNamesToMeasurementComplexDataMap maps the measurePropNames properties to its list of complex data.

The following errors may be returned:

* 185047 – The given object type is not supported for complex data. The supported types are "Att0MeasurableAttribute" and "Att0AttributeDefRevision".

* 185050 – The property does not exist on Att0MeasurableAttribute, please provide valid property name.
Operation.useCase: The System Engineer opens Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute in Active Workspace and reviews the complex data (if existing) for Goal or Min or Max properties.

ParameterInfo.description: The criteria for filtering the list of latest ItemRevision objects.

Operation.description: This operation gets the list of ItemRevision objects based on given criteria. The DefinitionCriteria holds criteria such as item revision name, item or item revision type name, enabled release status as well as disabled release status; if and only if the latest ItemRevision or sub type objects will be returned when it matches these criteria.
Operation.name: getLatestDefinitions
Operation.returnDescription:
A map of client identifier to the ItemRevision objects .The following partial errors may be returned:
    • 126101     When value of itemOrRevType of DefinitionCriteria structure is empty.
    • 126102     When value of itemOrRevType of DefinitionCriteria structure does not exist.    
    • 126103     When value of itemOrRevType of DefinitionCriteria structure is not item or item revision type.
Operation.useCase:
Creates an Attribute Definition Revision into Teamcenter like below and following story:
{ItemID=000001, Name=AttributeDef_1, Revision=B, Release Status="Released"}
{ItemID=000001, Name=AttributeDef_1, Revision=C, Release Status="Obsolete"}
{ItemID=000001, Name=AttributeDef_1, Revision=D, Release Status=""}

When DefinitionCriteria is set as objectName=AttributeDef_1, itemOrRevType=Att0AttributeDefRevision enabledStatus=Released, disabledStatus=Obsolete

At first case, revision B will be returned since it is released and status matches "Released"
At second case, none revision is returned since latest revision C is "Obsolete"
At third case, none revision is returned since latest revision D is in progress, while revision C is obsoleted

ParameterInfo.description: Information needed to create or update the complex data for the given Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute.

Operation.description: This operation sets the complex data for the given Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute.
Operation.name: setAttributeComplexData
Operation.returnDescription:
Standard ServiceData object to hold the partial errors that the operation encounters.
The following errors may be returned:

185047 – The given object type is not supported for complex data. The supported types are "Att0MeasurableAttribute" and "Att0AttributeDefRevision".
185048 - No complex data was provided.
185049 - The file type is invalid for complex data. The supported types are "xlsx" and "xlsm".
185050 – The property does not exist for objects of type Attribute Table (Att0AttributeTable) and Value Table (Att0ValueTable).
185051 – The number of rows/colums are different across Min/Max/Goal/Measurement tables. The import of complex data will proceed nevertheless.
185052 – The column definition is different across Min/Max/Goal/Measurement tables. The import of complex data will proceed nevertheless.
185053 – The number of rows/colums are different across Min/Max/Goal/Measurement tables. The import of complex data will not proceed.
185054 – The Column definition is different across Min/Max/Goal/Measurement tables. The import of complex data will not proceed.
Operation.useCase:
Use case 1:
The System Engineer can import the Excel file into the given Attribute Definition. In the background, the system extracts the table content from the Excel and then update the array of data to the Attribute Definition.

Use case 2:
The System Engineer opens the Attribute Summary page which may display the Goal/Min/Max table if existing and start edit and click “Save” to update the array of data to the Attribute.
Blow is the sample input structure, in the form of XML, that the caller of this service operation needs to provider for below table.

Below is the sample input structure, in the form of XML, that the caller of this service operation needs to provider for below table.

Time Temperature
Temp 1800 25.4
Temp2 1900 22.2

<SetAttributeComplexDataIn>
<clientId>NXModeling”</clientId>
<isProceedWithValidationError>true</isProceedWithValidationError>
<objectToModify>objectTag</objectToModify>
<propNameToTableDataMap map_key="Att0MinTable">
<map_value>
<AttrTabeData>
<opeation>"Add"</operation>
<AttrComplexData>
<tableName>”TempratureRecord”</tableName>
<columnHeaders>
<AttrColumnHeader>
<index>1</index>
<name>Time</name>

</AttrColumnHeader>
<AttrColumnHeader>
<index>2</index>
<name>Temperature</name>
</AttrColumnHeader>
</columnHeaders>
<rowHeaders>
<AttrRowHeader>
<index>11</index>
<name>Temp1</name>
</AttrRowHeader>
<AttrRowHeader>
<index>22</index>
<name>Temp2</name>
</AttrRowHeader>
</rowHeaders>
<rows>
<AttrTableRow>
<Index>11</Index>
<cells>
<AttrTableCell>
<Index>1</Index>
<Data>1800</Data>
</AttrTableCell>
<AttrTableCell>
<Index>2</Index>
<Data>25.4</Data>
</AttrTableCell>
</cells>
<AttrTableRow>
<Index>22</Index>
<cells>
<AttrTableCell>
<Index>1</Index>
<Data>1900</Data>
</AttrTableCell>
<AttrTableCell>
<Index>2</Index>
<Data>22.2</Data>
</AttrTableCell>
</cells>
</AttrTableRow>
</rows>
</AttrComplexData>
</AttrTabeData>
<map_value>
</propNameToTableDataMap>

Operation.description: Set properties from revise input Operation
Operation.name: setPropertiesFromReviseInput

Operation.description:
This operation is used to synchronize Att0MeasurableAttribute objects between source Att0MeasurableAttribute instances and target Att0MeasurableAttribute instances. This synchronization operation has two process flows: synchronize from source Att0MeasurableAttribute instances to input Att0MeasurableAttribute instances; and publish from output Att0MeasurableAttribute instances to source Att0MeasurableAttribute instances.
    • When synchronize from source to input Att0MeasurableAttribute, an update operation is done against input Att0MeasurableAttribute with latest property values from source Att0MeasurableAttribute. Meanwhile copies the latest Att0MeasurableValue of source Att0MeasurableAttribute and inserts it into the input Att0MeasurableAttribute.
    • When publish from output to source Att0MeasurableAttribute, the latest Att0MeasurableValue is retrieved from output Att0MeasurableAttribute and inserts it into the latest Att0MeasurableValue list on the source Att0MeasurableAttribute. The source Att0MeasurableAttribute properties will not be updated.
    • If there is an issue during publication, the process stops and all modified objects are be restored.
Operation.name: synchronizeMeasuableAttributes
Operation.returnDescription:
A map of client identifier to the Att0MeasurableAttribute objects. The following partial errors may be returned:
    • 185017 - The given object is invalid primary object for Input Measurable Attribute.
    • 185018 - The given object is invalid primary object for Output Measurable Attribute.
    • 185025 - Some input Att0MeasurableAttribute objects are out of date, publication could only be done when all input Att0MeasurableAttribute objects are up to date.
    • 185026 - The given synchronization candidates type does not match to the source object type.
    • 185027 - The validation object is not allowed to publish measurable attributes, because its release status is not match with pre-defined ones.
Operation.useCase:
1.    A system architect creates a product structure of logical blocks (Fnd0LogicalBlock), and defines for each block its measurable attributes (Att0MeasurableAttribute). As the design of the product architecture evolves, the system architect requests a partial or complete validation of the system. As part of the request system architect defines the logical blocks to be validated, their attributes that will inputs for the validation and the ones that need to be measured. As the system architect sets a logical block attribute (Att0MeasurableAttribute) as an input of the validation, the system builds a copy of the attribute for the validation context. The goal is to isolate the validation context from any updates that can be done on the product context.
However when processing the validation request, the validation engineer should know if the input attributes are up to date with the product logical blocks attributes. If they are not the validation engineer can run synchronize input attributes command.
2.    A system architect creates a product structure of logical blocks (Fnd0LogicalBlock), and defines for each block its measurable attributes (Att0MeasurableAttribute). As the design of the product architecture evolves, the system architect requests a partial or complete validation of the system. As part of the request system architect defines the logical blocks to be validated, their attributes that will inputs for the validation and the ones that need to be measured. As the system architect sets a logical block attribute (Att0MeasurableAttribute) as an input of the validation, the system builds a copy of the attribute for the validation context. The goal is to isolate the validation context from any updates that can be done on the product context.
However when processing the validation request, the validation engineer should know if the output attributes are measured which comes out a new measurement value (Att0MeasureValue). If new measurement value is generated, the validation engineer can run publish command to publish output attribute measurement value to logical block attribute.

    • 185208 - The measurable attribute could not be deleted, because it is referenced.

ServiceInterface.name: AttributeTargetManagement

StructElement.description: The FMS ticket for the Excel file to be imported.
StructElement.name: fileTicket

StructElement.description: A map (string, string) where the key is the name of the tab in the Excel and the value is the BMIDE property defined in the class Attr0AttributeTable to write complex data on.
StructElement.name: sheetNamePropNameMapping

StructElement.description: A map (string, string) where the key is the name of the tab in the Excel and and the value is the BMIDE property defined in the class Attr0ValueTable to write complex data on.
StructElement.name: sheetNameMeasurementPropNameMapping

Struct.description: Represents all the data needed to import complex data from Excel.
Struct.name: AttrFileData

StructElement.description: The header id of the column. It should match with the Index property of column header.
StructElement.name: index

StructElement.description: The actual data to be stored. Data will be always be stored in string format, client need to convert the data to the data type according to type of Attribute.
StructElement.name: data

Struct.description: Represents a cell in the column.
Struct.name: AttrTableCell

StructElement.description: clientId Identifier defined by client to track the related object.
StructElement.name: clientId

StructElement.description: The object from which complex data on its properties need to be retrieved.
StructElement.name: objectToRetrieve

StructElement.description: The Att0Attributetable property names on which the complex data need to be retrieved for the given object.
StructElement.name: attrPropNames

StructElement.description: The Att0ValueTable property names of Measurements on which the complex data need to be retrieved for the given object.
StructElement.name: measurePropNames

Struct.description: This operation gets the complex data for the given Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute.
Struct.name: GetAttributeComplexDataIn

StructElement.description: The object from which complex data on its properties need to be retrieved.
StructElement.name: objectToRetrieve

StructElement.description: A map of name of attribute property and its AttributeComplexData to be read from objectToRetrieve.
StructElement.name: attrPropNamesToAttrComplexDataMap

StructElement.description: A map of name of measure property and its AttributeComplexData to be read from objectToRetrieve.
StructElement.name: measurePropNamesToMeasurementComplexDataMap

Struct.description: GetAttributeComplexDataOutput structure represents all the information needed to retrieve complex data for properties on Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute.
Struct.name: GetAttributeComplexDataOutput

StructElement.description: The ServiceData with partial errors for GetAttributeComplexDataInput identified by its clientID.
StructElement.name: serviceData

StructElement.description: attributeComplexDataOutput represents all the information needed to retrieve complex data for properties on Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute.
StructElement.name: attributeComplexDataOutput

Struct.description: GetAttributeComplexDataInput structure represents all the information needed to retrieve complex data for properties on Attribute Definition or Measurable Attribute.
Struct.name: GetAttributeComplexDataResp

StructElement.description: This unique ID is used to identify return data elements and partial errors associated with this input structure.
StructElement.name: clientId

StructElement.description: The object on which complex data needs to be created or updated. The allowed types are: Att0AttributeDefRevision and Att0MeasurableAttribute.
StructElement.name: objectToModify

StructElement.description: Stores all the data related to import of complex data from Excel. If user has provided value of ‘propNameToComplexDataMap’ or ‘propNameToMeasurementComplexDataMap’ then value of this parameter is not required.
StructElement.name: fileData

StructElement.description: Name value pair (string, AttrTableData) of name of property and list of AttrTableData to be written to objectToModify. The property name is Teamcenter internal name of property on Att0AttributeTable. Supported properties are: att0GoalTable, att0MinTable and att0MaxTable.
StructElement.name: propNameToTableDataMap

StructElement.description: Name value pair (string, AttrTableData) of name of property list of AttrTableData to be written to to be written to objectToModify. The property name is Teamcenter internal name of property on Att0ValueTable. Supported property is: att0ValueTable.
StructElement.name: propNameToMeasurementTableDataMap

StructElement.description: If true, system will proceed with import of data and show warning to user if there are any validation errors in the input data. If false, system will show the validation error and will not import the data.
StructElement.name: isProceedWithValidationError

Struct.description: Represents all the information needed to create or update the complex data on Attribute Definition and Measurable Attribute.
Struct.name: SetAttributeComplexDataIn

StructElement.description: Identifier defined by client to track the related object.
StructElement.name: clientID

StructElement.description:
The parent object holds list of measurable attributes which need to be synchonized.
    • The parent object type must be defined in "PLE_InputOutputAttrParentObjectTypes" preference.
StructElement.name: parentObj

StructElement.description:
A list of Att0MeasurableAttribute object which needs to be synzhonized or published.
    • If this parameter is provided, system will compare candidates with their source Att0MeasurableAttribute object and check if they are are out-of-date; if the candidate is out-of-date system only synchonize/publish these candidates.
StructElement.name: attrCandidates

Struct.description: SyncMeasurableAttributeInput structure represents the criteria to synchronize measurable attribute objects.
Struct.name: SyncMeasurableAttributeInput

StructElement.description: The SyncDirection which identify the synchornization direction.
StructElement.name: direction

StructElement.description:
A list of relation names from where system traverse a list of input Att0MeasurableAttribute objects for synchronize.
    • If SyncDirection is SyncFromSource, this parameter is used along with parentObj to find a list of Att0MeasurableAttribute which will be updated with latest values from source Att0MeasurableAttribute as well as latest Att0MeasurableValue related to each Att0MeasurableAttribute.
    • If SyncDirection is PublishToSource, this parameter is used along with parentObj to find a list of input Att0MeasurableAttribute objects. If input Att0MeasurableAttribute objects are out of date, system stops publication.
StructElement.name: inputRelations

StructElement.description:
A list of relation names from where system traverse a list of outputAtt0MeasurableAttribute objects for synchronization.
    • If SyncDirection is SyncFromSource, this parameter is used along with parentObj to find a list of output Att0MeasurableAttribute. Then system removes Att0Measurement object for each output Att0MeasurableAttribute object.
    • If SyncDirection is PublishToSource, this parameter is used along with parentObj to find a list of output Att0MeasurableAttribute objects. System will insert the latest Att0MeasurableValue to source Att0MeasurableAttribute for each output Att0MeasurableAttribute object .
StructElement.name: outputRelations

Struct.description: SyncMeasurableAttributePref structure represents the synchronization direction(synchonize from source or publish to source), and input relations as well as output relations which are used to find out all input or output measurable attributes.
Struct.name: SyncMeasurableAttributePref

StructElement.description: The Service Data with partial errors identified by its clientId.
StructElement.name: serviceData

StructElement.description: Map input client ID to list of Att0MeasurableAttribute objects
StructElement.name: outputMap

Struct.description: SyncMeasurableAttributeResponse holds the response of synchronize Measuable Attributes. This output structure contains service data with partial errors and an outputMap which contains list of Att0MeasurableAttribute objects which have been synchronized.
Struct.name: SyncMeasurableAttributeResponse

StructElement.description: The operation to be performed on the complex data. Supported values are: “Add”, “Update”, and “Delete”.
StructElement.name: operation

StructElement.description: The actual complex data to perform operation on.
StructElement.name: complexData

Struct.description: Represent information about complex data and operation to be performend on the data.
Struct.name: AttrTableData

StructElement.description: The unique id for row or column in the table. It will be used by the client of this SOA to show column and row in the correct order and also to make any changes to the row or column.
StructElement.name: index

StructElement.description: Name of the row header or column header. This is an optional field.
StructElement.name: name

Struct.description: Represents header information for either row or column. The AttrTableHeader is generic data structure. If it is used in AttrTableRow then this represent information about row else if it is used in column headers then it represents information about column.
Struct.name: AttrTableHeader

StructElement.description: The header id of the row. It should match with the Index property of row header.
StructElement.name: index

StructElement.description: Information about various cells of a row which includes header information, its id and actual data.
StructElement.name: cells

Struct.description: Represents a single row of data.
Struct.name: AttrTableRow

StructElement.description: The name of table which represents the complex data.
StructElement.name: tableName

StructElement.description: A list of AttrTableHeader objects which has Information about various column headers which includes column name, its id and order.
StructElement.name: columnHeaders

StructElement.description: A list of AttrTableHeader objects which has information about various row headers which includes row name, its id and order.
StructElement.name: rowHeaders

StructElement.description: A list of AttrTableRow objects which represents the actual complex data in form of rows. Each row may have have one or more columns which holds the actual data.
StructElement.name: rows

Struct.description: Represents the complex data of a property. The complex data includes Table Name, Row Headers, Column Headers and actual data of 2D array. The column headers and row headers are optional.
Struct.name: AttributeComplexData

StructElement.description: Identifier defined by client to track the related object.
StructElement.name: clientID

StructElement.description: Required. Item or item revision type name which must exist in database.
StructElement.name: itemOrRevType

StructElement.description: Optional.The item revision name which is used to perform the search. Wild card "*" is supported.
StructElement.name: objectName

StructElement.description:
Optional. A list of allowed release status names. During the search system checks the latest ItemRevision release status name, if the status name is contained in enabledStatus, then the ItemRevision object will be returned.
    • If enabledStatus is empty , the latest ItemRevision will be returned
    • If enabledStatus contains multiple values, any release status on latest ItemRevision that is matched will be returned
    • If the latest ItemRevision has multiple release statuses, only check against the newest release status.
StructElement.name: enabledStatus

StructElement.description:
A list of disallowed release status names. During the search system checks the latest ItemRevision's release status name, if the status name is contained in disabledStatus, the ItemRevision object will be ignored.
    • If disabledStatus is empty, the latest ItemRevision will be returned
    • If disabledStatus contains multiple values, any release status on latest ItemRevision that is matched will be ignored
    • If the latest ItemRevision has multiple release statuses, only check against the newest release status
StructElement.name: disabledStatus

Struct.description: Structure represents the criteria to get latest ItemRevision objects.
Struct.name: DefinitionCriteria

StructElement.description: The Service Data with partial errors for DefinitionCriteria identified by its clientId.
StructElement.name: serviceData

StructElement.description: Map of input client IDs to the list of ItemRevision objects.
StructElement.name: outputMap

Struct.description: Holds the response for getLatestDefinitions. This output structure contains service data with partial errors and a StrDefinitionMap which contains latest ItemRevision objects based on give criteria for each entry in the DefinitionCriteria input list.
Struct.name: DefinitionsResponse

StructElement.description: Identifier defined by client to track the related object.
StructElement.name: clientID

StructElement.description: The parent object for measurable attributes.
StructElement.name: parentObj

StructElement.description: A list of Att0MeasurableAttribute object which will be deleted from parent object.
StructElement.name: measurableAttributes

Struct.description: DeleteMeasurableAttributeInput structure represents list of measurable attributes to be deleted from parent object.
Struct.name: DeleteMeasurableAttributeInput

StructElement.description: The ServiceData with partial errors for DeletesMeasurableAttributeInput identified by its clientID.
StructElement.name: serviceData

StructElement.description: Represents list of un-deleted objects, identified by its clientID.
StructElement.name: failedToDeleteAttrTagMap

Struct.description: Holds the response of deleteMeasuableAttributes. This response structure contains un-deleted objects and service data with partial errors.
Struct.name: DeleteMeasurableAttributesResponse

Typedef.description: Contains clientID and list of input objects Att0MeasurableAttribute that could not be deleted by the system.
Typedef.name: FailedToDeleteAttrTagMap

Typedef.description: The map will hold a string as key and another string as value.
Typedef.name: NameValueMap

Typedef.description: This map will hold name of property as key and its corresponding complex data as value.
Typedef.name: PropertyToComplexDataMap

Typedef.description: This map will hold name of property as key and correspousing table data as value.
Typedef.name: PropertyToTableDataMap

Typedef.description: Contains client Id and the latest ItemRevision objects or its subtype which match search criteria.
Typedef.name: StrDefinitionsMap

Typedef.description:
SyncMeasurableAttributeMap contains client Id and Att0MeasurableAttribute objects which have been synchonized.
    • If SyncDirection is SyncFromSource, SyncMeasurableAttributeMap contains all input Att0MeasurableAttribute objects that been synchronized successfully.
    • If SyncDirection is PublishToSource, SyncMeasurableAttributeMap contains all source Att0MeasurableAttribute objects that been published successfully.
Typedef.name: SyncMeasurableAttributesMap
